codeReview.summary: Core ranking/capping logic is solid, but the final tie-break is reversed from the stated ID-ascending policy, so exact ties can keep different sessions under caps. The usage-summary date formatting is also locale-sensitive and not fully deterministic.

issues.description: Equal-score sessions are ordered with `tieBreakKey(a) > tieBreakKey(b)`, which makes the final ID tie-break descend lexicographically. The commit/commentary says the last tie-break should be stable ID ascending, so exact ties can select the wrong session when the 5-session cap or char trimming is applied.

Usage-summary formatting pins time zone but not locale/calendar, so the same pack can stringify differently on non-English or non-Gregorian machines. That makes the summary less deterministic than the rest of the ranking pipeline.

summary: Fail: exact-tie ordering is reversed from the declared policy.
